<Language Requirements> https://www.liverpool.ac.uk/international/applying/entry-requirements/english-language-requirements/
• Humanities and Social Sciences, Health and Life Sciences - TOEFL Minimum: 88
• Science and Engineering - TOEFL Minimum: 78
• TOEFL score accepted for UK Student visa: https://bit.ly/toefl_for_student_visa

• Academics: https://www.liverpool.ac.uk/global-opportunities/visiting-students/study-in-liverpool/what-can-i-study/
• All areas are open to exchange except Medicine, Veterinary Science, Dentistry, Health Sciences (e.g. Nursing, Physiotherapy etc) 
• Students are limited to two classes per semester from the School of Management, unless you are majoring in a business or management course at your home University.
All areas are open to exchange except Medicine, Veterinary Science, Dentistry, Health Sciences (e.g. Nursing, Physiotherapy etc)
R: 18 L: 17 S: 20 W: 17 (based on the minimum requirements for UK Visa)

• Health and Social Care/ Post Graduate modules not available to exchange students.  
• Creative Arts, including  Film and TV modules have extremerely limited availability. 
• Places on modules of the Film and TV programme are extremely limited and UH can only support applications from candidates who will spend the full academic year at UH starting from fall and have practical experience of film making (camera work, editing & post production). Evidence of such experience needs to be included in the application.
• modules with codes that contain ‘CEP’, ‘FBS’, ‘FTC’, ‘FDA’, ‘FCO’, ‘FHH’, ‘FEN’, ‘IFS’, ‘FHE’,‘(HIC)’, ‘AHP’, ‘FBS’, ‘FTC’, ‘FCO’, ‘FHH’, ‘FEN’, ‘IFS’, ‘HIC’, ‘FHE’, ‘NMH’
• Modules beginning with AHP or NMH

* Course catalogue: https://www.gla.ac.uk/study/visiting/studyabroadexchange/studyabroadprogramme/coursecatalogue/
* Students should read the application guidance notes and Course Selection Guide on the website carefully before selecting courses.
* Students can apply to study at either the main campus or the Dumfries Campus. Due to the distance between the two campuses, students cannot choose courses across both campuses.
* Students must choose specific courses and NOT general subject areas. Courses are listed on our course catalogue and students should refer to the Visiting Students Course Selection Guide when choosing. Students do not need to look at the pre-requisites on the course catalogue. These apply to degree-seeking students only.
* They can choose courses from multiple colleges across the University, regardless of which college they have selected on their application.
* Level 1 and 2 courses are lower-level courses that require no/little previous experience. Level 3 and 4 courses are upper-level courses that require at least two years background experience in the subject area that will be studied at Glasgow.

< Unavailable >
Medicine, Vet Medicine and Dentistry
level 5 courses in all subjects
Semester 1 only students are not permitted to take level 3 and 4 Computing Science, Maths or Stats courses due to course structure.
[For Spanish program applicants only]: minimum of B1 level of Spanish required. Home university certificates accepted

You can choose subjects from any course, with one exception. During the third year of some international programs, UPNA students must do exchanges at other universities. Because local students enrolled in third-year subjects in English take them abroad, only third-year subjects in Spanish are taught at the UPNA. If the subject available in English is taught in any other year, or is an elective, it is usually taught at the UPNA. It can be checked in the subject guide.
http://www.unavarra.es/relacionesinternacionales/relaciones-internacionales-y-movilidad/estudiantes-internacionales/course-catalogue+?languageId=1
Medicine is not open to exchange students
We recommend a B2-minimum level, in English or French, depending on the language of the selected courses.
The language level depends on the academic department. We recommend a B2-minimum level, in English or French, depending on the language of the selected courses.
[For Russian program applicants only]: B2 level of Russian is required for courses instructed in Russian. Submission of proof of language proficiency is not required.

Academic Offer: https://ifea.spbu.ru/en/non-degree/academic-offer
Please note that the list of courses can be changed thus before coming to SPbU students are advised to ask the coordinator of the host faculty about the particular courses availability: https://ifea.spbu.ru/en/contactsr/147-academic-mobility-department,-spsu-academic-office
Submission of proof of language proficiency is not required.

courses for exchange students and the study of the Russian language
https://iaas.msu.ru/education/obrazovatelnye-programmy/
[For French program applicants only]: A home university letter certifying his/her [French] proficiency in B2 level can be accepted for students taking courses in [French].

Students can choose any course from any programme as long as they choose courses from their current year, the year below and the year above.
https://www.iscparis.com/en/incoming-students/#block_621bb750305aa

•Expertise Audit Controle (taught in French) is not available to exchange students.
•Depending on the term some courses may not be offered. 
•ONLY PGE Program courses are available (Licence 3, Master 1 and Master 2 classes).
[For Russian program applicants only]: Submission of proof of language proficiency is not required. The candidate needs to have an average level of knowledge of the Russian language A2-B1.

* Programs in Russian are available in their entirety. 
https://www.dvfu.ru/en/academics/
* The programs of study that are provided in English are listed below: 
- School of Engineering 
“Construction”  
- School of Biomedicine 
“Biotechnology”, “Public health”, “Biology” 
- Institute of Oriental Studies – School of Regional and International Studies 
“International Relations” 
- School of Economics and Management 
“Management” 
- School of Education 
“Teacher Education” 
- School of Biomedicine 
“General Medicine”
We don't require any tests. We recommend the students to have English level B2.
Any field of studies provided that courses are included in the education offer published at www.ir.unibocconi.eu/coe
Undergraduate students can access undergraduate courses only.

changes.from:
* Restrictions: https://www.unige.ch/exchange/en/incoming/why-geneva/students/international-non-erasmus-students/2-admission-conditions/restrictions/
 - Courses may be chosen from a maximum of two different Faculties
 - Master's-level courses are restricted to postgraduate students and subject availability. Students in their fourth year of studies onwards and students enrolled in the Faculty of Law can contact our office.
 - Master's of Advanced Studies classes, continuing education programmes and research project classes are closed to exchange students.
 - Some seminars may be full or restricted
 - Certain courses may have prerequisites
 - Practical classes (TP) may be accessible upon request
 - Students who wish to attend regular courses are advised to have a sufficient command of French (level B2 and level B1 if taking courses in the École de langue et de civilisation française.
* Faculty-specific restrictions: https://www.unige.ch/exchange/files/2716/6249/1403/GUIDE_Study_PlanCourses_Restrictions_22-23_Monde.pdf

changes.from:
•        TOEFL (iBT) score of 80+: minimum sub score or 18 for reading & listening, 20 for speaking and 21 for writing
•        IELTS 65: minimum sub score of 6 0 for reading, listening, speaking, and writing
•        TOEIC 800+ for listening & reading AND 320+ for speaking & writing Both need to be presentedl
•        CEFR level B2
•        Cambridge FCE/CAE/CPE/BEC Higher 170: minimum sub score of 170 for reading, listening, speaking & writing
•        Another English language certificate of a comparable level with comparable (sub)scores

NOTE: we do not accept language statements from universities anymore, unless it is a statement that the entire programme is taught in English

changes.from:
https://studiekiezer.ugent.be/en/zoek

* Faculty of Economics and Business Administration,
* Faculty of Engineering and Architecture: Exchange program available for master level only. (https://www.ugent.be/ea/en/for-exchange-students)
* Faculty of Sciences, 
* Faculty of Psychology and Educational Sciences, 
* Faculty of Bioscience Engineering 
 - You should check the above faculties' exchange programs here: https://studiekiezer.ugent.be/en/zoek?zt=&aj=2024&otc=EXCH&voMa=&voPB=&voAB=&taal=EN
 - If the faculty offers master's level programs, students must have completed at least 90 Yonsei credits before the exchange semester starts, to take Master's level courses.
